result.summary: Found several visual layout issues, mostly related to horizontal clipping and fixed navigation overlap on mobile.

issues.description: Several content rows are clipped on the right edge of the screen. The third game card in multiple rows is only partially visible, and the top category item shows only part of the label, such as “Hot Ga…”. This may be intentional carousel behavior, but visually it looks like the page is overflowing horizontally.
issues.suggestion: If these are horizontal carousels, add clearer affordances such as proper spacing, fade edges, or scroll indicators. If not intentional, adjust the grid width and card sizing so content fits within the viewport.

issues.description: The fixed bottom navigation bar overlaps the game content near the bottom of the screen, partially covering the last visible row of game tiles.
issues.suggestion: Add bottom padding or safe-area spacing to the page content equal to the height of the fixed bottom nav so cards are not hidden underneath it.

issues.description: The category navigation row near the top appears horizontally cut off, with the “Hot Games” pill partially visible and truncated at the right edge.
issues.suggestion: Ensure the horizontal category scroller has comfortable side padding and a clear scroll cue, or reduce pill widths so more items fit cleanly on screen.

issues.description: Some small overlay icons, especially the black circular “i” info icons on game tiles, may be hard to see against dark or busy game artwork.
issues.suggestion: Increase icon size, contrast, or add a stronger background treatment to make the controls consistently visible.
